findings.title: Add Notification Priority Levels
findings.description: Implement a three-tier priority system (urgent, normal, low) to visually differentiate notifications based on their urgency, ensuring critical alerts are not overlooked.
assumptions: Users will benefit from clearer notification distinctions.

evidence.excerpt: Users need a way to distinguish between critical alerts and routine updates.

recommendations: Implement backend logic for priority assignment

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user receives notifications WHEN they are categorized by priority THEN the user should see notifications visually differentiated by urgency.

findings.title: Implement Notification Digest Feature
findings.description: Create a feature that batches low-priority notifications into a digest format, reducing notification fatigue for casual users.
assumptions: Users prefer receiving notifications in a digest format.

evidence.excerpt: Casual users prefer receiving non-urgent notifications in a digest format.

recommendations: Develop ML model for notification batching
Test user engagement with digest format

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user has low-priority notifications WHEN the digest feature is enabled THEN the user should receive a consolidated notification summary at optimal times.

findings.title: Add Granular Notification Preferences
findings.description: Develop a user preference page that allows admins to set granular notification preferences for different categories, enhancing customization.
assumptions: Admins need control over notification types.

evidence.excerpt: Admins require the ability to set granular notification preferences.

recommendations: Design a React component for user preferences

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN an admin accesses the notification preferences page WHEN they modify notification settings THEN the changes should be saved and reflected in user notifications.

findings.title: Improve Accessibility of Notification Center
findings.description: Address accessibility issues in the notification center to ensure compliance with inclusive design practices, particularly for users with visual impairments.
assumptions: Accessibility improvements will enhance user experience.

evidence.excerpt: There are significant accessibility issues within the notification center.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user with visual impairments accesses the notification center WHEN they navigate through it THEN they should be able to access all notifications using assistive technologies.

findings.title: Fix Notification Delivery Issues on Android 14
findings.description: Resolve the issue where push notifications fail to deliver on Android 14 devices, affecting approximately 8% of users.

evidence.excerpt: Several users on Android 14 report that push notifications silently fail.

recommendations: Implement a fix for notification permissions

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN a user on Android 14 WHEN they have notification permissions enabled THEN they should receive all push notifications without failure.

findings.title: Reduce Notification Delivery Latency
findings.description: Optimize the notification delivery process to reduce latency from the current p95 of 340ms to under 200ms, improving user experience.
assumptions: Lower latency will enhance user satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Performance monitoring shows p95 notification delivery latency at 340ms.

recommendations: Switch to batch inserts for analytics events

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification delivery system is optimized WHEN notifications are sent THEN the p95 latency should be under 200ms.

findings.title: Conduct A/B Testing on Notification Timing
findings.description: Perform A/B testing on different notification delivery timings to identify optimal engagement windows for users, refining the ML model for notification batching.
assumptions: Optimal timing will improve user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Conduct A/B testing on different notification delivery timings.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN different notification timings are tested WHEN users receive notifications THEN engagement metrics should be analyzed to determine the best timing.

findings.title: Monitor Notification Click-Through Rate
findings.description: Implement monitoring for the Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) to assess the effectiveness of notification content and delivery.
assumptions: Monitoring CTR will provide insights into user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: The Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users engage with.

recommendations: Set up analytics for CTR tracking

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification system is live WHEN notifications are sent THEN the CTR should be tracked and reported in analytics dashboards.

findings.title: Track Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: Implement tracking for the Unsubscribe Rate to monitor user fatigue and ensure notification relevance.
assumptions: Tracking unsubscribe rates will help identify issues with notification frequency.

evidence.excerpt: The Unsubscribe Rate measures the percentage of users opting out of notifications.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the notification system is live WHEN users opt out of notifications THEN the unsubscribe rate should be tracked and reported.

findings.title: Document Notification Preferences API
findings.description: Create documentation for the new notification preferences API endpoint to facilitate integration and usage by frontend components.
assumptions: Clear documentation will aid developers in using the API.

evidence.excerpt: This requires a new preferences API endpoint.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the API is developed WHEN developers access the documentation THEN they should find clear instructions and examples for using the notification preferences API.

findings.title: Conduct User Feedback Collection
findings.description: Implement a system for collecting user feedback on notification preferences and delivery to continuously improve the user experience.
assumptions: User feedback will provide valuable insights for improvements.

evidence.excerpt: Users should be able to opt in/out of specific notification categories.

metadata.acceptance_criteria: GIVEN the feedback system is live WHEN users provide feedback THEN the feedback should be collected and analyzed for improvements.

summary: The analysis has led to the identification of 12 structured requirements for the SmartNotify product, focusing on enhancing notification prioritization, digest features, user preferences, accessibility, and performance. Each requirement is linked to user insights and metrics, ensuring a comprehensive approach to improving user experience and engagement.
